title: Hidden in Plain Sight
description: A suspicious image file `nexus_logo.png` was intercepted. Examination of the raw bytes shows non-PNG data appended after the IEND marker. Running `strings` on it reveals a hidden message. What does it say?
hint: Run `strings` on the file and look for strings that appear after the normal PNG footer. PNG files end with IEND.

title: Log Archaeology
description: The `/var/log/auth.log` shows a brute-force attack. Analyze the timestamps to determine how many failed login attempts were made for user 'root' before the successful authentication.
hint: Use `grep 'Failed password' auth.log | grep 'root' | wc -l` to count failed attempts.

title: Deleted But Not Gone
description: A file was deleted from `/tmp/` but the inode data remains in the filesystem journal. The filename started with `.exfil_` and was 847 bytes. What command would you use to recover it from an ext4 journal?
hint: The `extundelete` tool can recover recently deleted files from ext3/ext4 filesystems by examining journal entries.

title: Steganographic Payload
description: A PNG image was used to exfiltrate data using LSB steganography. The image is `chimera_note.png`. The steghide passphrase is stored in the EXIF data under the 'Comment' field. What tool extracts EXIF metadata?
hint: ExifTool reads metadata from image files including hidden comments. After finding the passphrase, use steghide to extract the payload.
